observations.title: Refactor pos2 to Clean Architecture + shadcn
observations.content:
# Proposal: pos2 Clean Architecture Refactor

## Intent

Refactor pos2 from vanilla JS with informal layering to a proper Clean Architecture implementation matching pos. Align with SOLID principles and established design patterns. Add TypeScript and shadcn/ui.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Migrate from vanilla JS to TypeScript
- Implement proper layer boundaries: domain/application/infrastructure/presentation
- Add repository interfaces in domain, implementations in infrastructure
- Add dependency injection via React Context
- Add use-cases in application layer
- Replace vanilla store with Zustand + TanStack Query
- Add shadcn/ui components
- Preserve existing UI/UX behavior

### Out of Scope
- Backend changes (pos2 connects to same API)
- New features

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `clean-architecture`: Full layered architecture with dependency rule
- `typescript`: Type-safe codebase
- `shadcn-ui`: Reusable component library
- `zustand-store`: Reactive state management
- `tanstack-query`: Server state management

### Modified Capabilities
- None (preserve existing behavior)

## Approach

1. Bootstrap React + TypeScript + Vite in pos2
2. Apply pos architecture: domain/entities, application/use-cases, infrastructure/repositories, presentation/components
3. Implement repository interfaces + concrete implementations
4. Add use-cases for business operations
5. Set up DI via Context
6. Add Zustand for client state
7. Add TanStack Query hooks
8. Replace UI with shadcn components

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `pos2/src/*` | Replaced | Full codebase restructure |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Behavior regression | Low | Compare UI outputs before/after |
| shadcn integration | Low | Use existing pos as reference |

## Rollback Plan

Keep backup of current pos2/src. Revert package.json + vite.config.js if needed.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] pos2 compiles without errors
- [ ] Same API calls as original
- [ ] Clean layer boundaries verified
- [ ] shadcn components render correctly

observations.title: Specs: pos2 Clean Architecture Refactor
observations.content:
# Delta for pos2 Clean Architecture Refactor

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Layered Architecture

The pos2 codebase SHALL follow Clean Architecture with four distinct layers: Domain, Application, Infrastructure, and Presentation. Each layer SHALL have specific responsibilities and clear boundaries.

#### Scenario: Layer Dependencies

- GIVEN a new feature request
- WHEN the architect designs the solution
- THEN dependencies MUST flow inward only (Domain → Application → Infrastructure → Presentation)
- AND Domain layer MUST NOT depend on any external framework or library

#### Scenario: Domain Layer Isolation

- GIVEN the Domain layer
- WHEN implementing business logic
- THEN it MUST contain only entities, value objects, repository interfaces, and domain exceptions
- AND MUST have zero external dependencies (no React, no axios, no fetch)

#### Scenario: Application Layer Use Cases

- GIVEN the Application layer
- WHEN orchestrating business operations
- THEN it MUST contain only use cases that depend on domain interfaces
- AND each use case MUST represent a single business action

#### Scenario: Infrastructure Layer Adapters

- GIVEN the Infrastructure layer
- WHEN implementing repository interfaces
- THEN it MUST contain only concrete implementations (API clients, repositories)
- AND it MUST transform domain entities to/from external formats

#### Scenario: Presentation Layer UI

- GIVEN the Presentation layer
- WHEN building user interfaces
- THEN it MUST contain only React components, hooks, store, and context
- AND it MUST depend on Application layer for business logic

### Requirement: TypeScript Strict Mode

The pos2 codebase MUST be migrated to TypeScript with strict type checking enabled. All code MUST pass TypeScript compilation without errors.

#### Scenario: TypeScript Compilation

- GIVEN running `tsc --noEmit`
- WHEN any TypeScript file contains type errors
- THEN the compilation MUST fail with clear error messages
- AND all domain, application, and infrastructure files MUST be `.ts`

#### Scenario: Domain Type Definitions

- GIVEN domain entities (Client, Product, Invoice, InvoiceDetail, Tax)
- WHEN defining their properties
- THEN each property MUST have explicit type annotations
- AND value objects MUST be immutable (readonly properties or const assertions)

### Requirement: Repository Pattern

The system MUST implement the Repository pattern with abstract interfaces in Domain and concrete implementations in Infrastructure.

#### Scenario: Product Repository Interface

- GIVEN `ProductRepository` interface in domain
- WHEN implementing the concrete repository
- THEN it MUST be located in `infrastructure/repositories/`
- AND it MUST implement all methods defined in the interface
- AND it MUST handle API calls to `/products` endpoint

#### Scenario: Client Repository Interface

- GIVEN `ClientRepository` interface in domain
- WHEN implementing the concrete repository
- THEN it MUST implement `getAll()` and `getById(id)` methods
- AND it MUST handle API calls to `/clients` endpoint

#### Scenario: Invoice Repository Interface

- GIVEN `InvoiceRepository` interface in domain
- WHEN implementing the concrete repository
- THEN it MUST implement `create(invoice)`, `update(invoice)`, and `getById(id)` methods
- AND it MUST handle API calls to `/invoices` endpoint

### Requirement: Use Cases

The system MUST implement use cases in the Application layer that orchestrate business logic. Each use case MUST represent a single business operation.

#### Scenario: AddItemToInvoiceUseCase

- GIVEN an invoice and a product to add
- WHEN `AddItemToInvoiceUseCase.execute(invoiceId, productId, quantity)` is called
- THEN it MUST validate stock availability
- AND it MUST add the item to invoice details
- AND it MUST recalculate totals

#### Scenario: UpdateItemQuantityUseCase

- GIVEN an invoice detail and new quantity
- WHEN `UpdateItemQuantityUseCase.execute(invoiceId, detailId, quantity)` is called
- THEN it MUST validate the new quantity against available stock
- AND it MUST update the item quantity
- AND it MUST recalculate the subtotal

#### Scenario: RemoveItemFromInvoiceUseCase

- GIVEN an invoice and detail item to remove
- WHEN `RemoveItemFromInvoiceUseCase.execute(invoiceId, detailId)` is called
- THEN it MUST remove the item from invoice details
- AND it MUST recalculate totals

#### Scenario: FinalizeInvoiceUseCase

- GIVEN a completed invoice with at least one item
- WHEN `FinalizeInvoiceUseCase.execute(invoiceId)` is called
- THEN it MUST validate the invoice is not empty
- AND it MUST persist the invoice via repository
- AND it SHALL return the finalized invoice with server-assigned ID

### Requirement: Dependency Injection via Context

The system MUST use React Context for dependency injection, providing repositories and use cases to components without tight coupling.

#### Scenario: RepositoryContext

- GIVEN `RepositoryContext` in presentation layer
- WHEN a component needs repository access
- THEN it MUST use `useRepository()` hook to access repositories
- AND repositories MUST be injected at app initialization

#### Scenario: ApplicationContext

- GIVEN `ApplicationContext` in presentation layer
- WHEN a component needs use case access
- THEN it MUST use `useApplication()` hook to access use cases
- AND use cases MUST receive repository instances via constructor

### Requirement: Zustand State Management

The system MUST use Zustand for client-side state management. The store MUST hold current invoice state and selected client.

#### Scenario: POS Store State

- GIVEN `usePOSStore` in presentation layer
- WHEN managing invoice state
- THEN it MUST hold `currentInvoice`, `selectedClient`
- AND it MUST provide `setInvoice()`, `setSelectedClient()`, `clear()` actions

#### Scenario: Store Subscriptions

- GIVEN components subscribed to the store
- WHEN state changes via `setInvoice()` or `setSelectedClient()`
- THEN all subscribed components MUST re-render with updated state

### Requirement: TanStack Query Hooks

The system MUST use TanStack Query for server state management. All API data fetching MUST go through typed query hooks.

#### Scenario: useProducts Hook

- GIVEN `useProducts()` hook
- WHEN fetching product list
- THEN it MUST use TanStack Query's `useQuery` with key `['products']`
- AND it MUST call `productRepository.getAll()`

#### Scenario: useClients Hook

- GIVEN `useClients()` hook
- WHEN fetching client list
- THEN it MUST use TanStack Query's `useQuery` with key `['clients']`
- AND it MUST call `clientRepository.getAll()`

#### Scenario: useInvoices Hook

- GIVEN `useInvoices()` hook
- WHEN fetching or creating invoices
- THEN it MUST provide `useQuery` for listing and `useMutation` for creation
- AND mutations MUST call `invoiceRepository.create()`

### Requirement: Shadcn UI Components

The system MUST use shadcn/ui components for the presentation layer, replacing vanilla HTML with accessible, themed components.

#### Scenario: UI Component Library

- GIVEN the shadcn/ui component library
- WHEN building POS UI
- THEN components MUST include: Button, Card, Dialog, Popover, Badge, Input, Label, Separator
- AND all components MUST be located in `src/components/ui/`

#### Scenario: Component Styling

- GIVEN shadcn/ui components with Tailwind CSS
- WHEN applying styles
- THEN they MUST use CSS variables from `index.css`
- AND they MUST be dark-mode ready via CSS variable `var(--background)`

### Requirement: Value Objects

The system MUST implement immutable value objects in the Domain layer with internal validation.

#### Scenario: Money Value Object

- GIVEN `Money` value object with `amount` and `currency`
- WHEN creating a Money instance
- THEN the amount MUST be a non-negative number
- AND operations (add, multiply) MUST return new Money instances
- AND the value object MUST NOT expose setters

#### Scenario: StockQuantity Value Object

- GIVEN `StockQuantity` value object
- WHEN creating a StockQuantity instance
- THEN the quantity MUST be a non-negative integer
- AND decrement operations MUST validate stock > 0

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Project File Structure

The pos2 project file structure MUST be reorganized from vanilla JS to TypeScript Clean Architecture.
(Previously: vanilla JS with informal layered structure)

#### Scenario: Source Directory Organization

- GIVEN the pos2/src directory after refactor
- WHEN listing the directory
- THEN it MUST contain: domain/, application/, infrastructure/, presentation/, components/, lib/
- AND each layer MUST contain its respective subdirectories

#### Scenario: Build Configuration

- GIVEN the pos2 project after refactor
- WHEN running `npm run build`
- THEN it MUST compile TypeScript via `tsc -b && vite build`
- AND it MUST produce optimized production build

observations.title: Updated Design: pos2 Vanilla JS Clean Architecture
observations.content:
# Design: pos2 Clean Architecture Refactor (Vanilla JS)

## Technical Approach

Migrate pos2 from vanilla JS layered architecture to proper Clean Architecture with vanilla JS patterns. Use Signals for reactive state management, shadcn-style CSS without React.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Signals for State Management

**Choice**: @preact/signals-core for reactive state
**Alternatives considered**: Pub/Sub store, Redux, MobX, Jotai
**Rationale**: Signals provide granular reactivity — only components subscribed to specific signals re-render. More efficient than pub/sub which typically triggers full re-renders. Zero dependencies, tiny footprint (~1KB).

### Decision: Reactive Components Pattern

**Choice**: Components as factory functions returning render functions that subscribe to signals
**Alternatives considered**: Class-based components, template literals
**Rationale**: Components auto-update when signals change. Pattern is clear and maintainable for vanilla JS.

### Decision: Event-Driven Component Communication

**Choice**: Custom events for component-to-component communication
**Rationale**: Decoupled components that communicate via events instead of direct method calls.

### Decision: shadcn-style CSS Without React

**Choice**: Copy CSS variables, utility classes, and HTML structure patterns from shadcn
**Alternatives considered**: Flowbite, plain Bootstrap
**Rationale**: User requested shadcn-style. We replicate the visual design system without the React components.

## Data Flow

```
User Action → Component Event Handler → Use Case (Application) → Domain Logic
                                                              ↓
DOM Update ← Signal Change ← Repository (Infrastructure) ← API
```

## File Changes

### Phase 1: Bootstrap
- `pos2/package.json` — Add signals, axios, tailwindcss
- `pos2/vite.config.ts` — Update for vanilla JS
- `pos2/index.html` — Mount point

### Phase 2: Domain Layer (TypeScript, vanilla)
- `pos2/src/domain/entities/*.ts` — Client, Product, Invoice, InvoiceDetail, Tax
- `pos2/src/domain/value-objects/*.ts` — Money, StockQuantity, Email
- `pos2/src/domain/repositories/*.ts` — Abstract interfaces
- `pos2/src/domain/exceptions/*.ts` — DomainException hierarchy

### Phase 3: Application Layer
- `pos2/src/application/use-cases/*.ts` — AddItem, UpdateQuantity, RemoveItem, FinalizeInvoice

### Phase 4: Infrastructure Layer
- `pos2/src/infrastructure/api/api-client.ts` — Fetch-based API client
- `pos2/src/infrastructure/repositories/*.ts` — Concrete implementations
- `pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/*.ts` — DTO mappers

### Phase 5: Presentation Layer (Vanilla JS)
- `pos2/src/presentation/store/signals.ts` — @preact/signals-core store
- `pos2/src/presentation/components/*.js` — Component factory functions
- `pos2/src/presentation/pages/*.js` — Page composition

### Phase 6: UI Layer
- `pos2/src/styles/index.css` — shadcn CSS variables, utility classes
- `pos2/src/components/ui/*.html` — shadcn-style HTML templates

## State Management Pattern (Signals)

```javascript
// signals.js
import { signal, computed } from '@preact/signals-core';

export const currentInvoice = signal(null);
export const selectedClient = signal(null);
export const invoiceDetails = computed(() => currentInvoice.value?.details ?? []);

export const subtotal = computed(() => /* calculate from details */);
export const taxTotal = computed(() => /* calculate from details */);
export const total = computed(() => subtotal.value.add(taxTotal.value));
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Unit | Domain entities, value objects, use cases | Vitest |
| Integration | Repositories with mock API | Vitest + MSW |

## Open Questions

- [x] Signals chosen as state management
- [x] shadcn-style CSS confirmed
- [ ] Keep vanilla JS structure or organize differently?

observations.title: Session summary: pos-api
observations.content:
## Goal
Refactor pos2 from vanilla JS layered architecture to proper Clean Architecture with TypeScript, Signals for state management, and shadcn-style CSS

## Instructions
- pos2 is vanilla JS (NOT React like pos)
- Use @preact/signals-core for reactive state management (best pattern for vanilla)
- Keep vanilla JS but apply shadcn-style CSS variables and utility classes
- Repository interfaces in TypeScript (.ts), implementations can be .js or .ts
- Build must pass: `npm run build`

## Discoveries
- pos2 already had a pub/sub store pattern - replaced with Signals for granular reactivity
- Axios interceptor in api-client extracts `response.data.data` - repositories need type casting since the interceptor already unwraps
- shadcn/ui is React-specific - user wanted shadcn-STYLE CSS only (CSS variables, utility classes)
- Vite 6 requires `.ts` extension for files using TypeScript `implements` keyword in the same build

## Accomplished
- ✅ Bootstrap: package.json (signals, axios), vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, index.html, CSS variables
- ✅ Domain Layer: 4 entities (Client, Product, Invoice, Tax), 3 value objects (Money, StockQuantity, Email), 4 repository interfaces, Domain exceptions
- ✅ Application Layer: 4 use cases (AddItem, UpdateQuantity, RemoveItem, FinalizeInvoice)
- ✅ Infrastructure Layer: API client, 4 mappers, 4 repository implementations
- ✅ Presentation Layer: Signals store, 3 components (ClientSelector, ProductGrid, CartView) with base-component pattern
- ✅ Build passes: `tsc -b && vite build` succeeds

## Relevant Files
- pos2/src/domain/ - TypeScript entities, value objects, repository interfaces
- pos2/src/application/use-cases/ - TypeScript use cases
- pos2/src/infrastructure/ - API client, repositories, mappers
- pos2/src/presentation/store/signals.js - @preact/signals-core store
- pos2/src/presentation/components/ - Vanilla JS components with signal subscriptions
- pos2/src/styles/index.css - shadcn-style CSS variables

observations.title: Product-Tax many-to-many relationship
observations.content:
**What**: Changed Product-Tax relationship from single to many-to-many using a join table `ProductTax`
**Why**: User requested products can have multiple taxes (e.g. IVA 21% + VAT 15%)
**Where**: pos-api/prisma/schema.prisma (new ProductTax model), pos-api/src/domain/entities/product.entity.ts (taxIds: number[]), pos-api/src/infrastructure/persistence/prisma/repositories/product.repository.ts, pos-api/src/infrastructure/persistence/prisma/mappers/product.mapper.ts, pos2/src/domain/entities/product.entity.ts, pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/product.mapper.ts, pos2/src/application/use-cases/add-item-to-invoice.use-case.ts
**Learned**: Prisma relation update syntax requires `{ connect: { id: x } }` for nested creates; `ts-node` seed needs proper type filtering for array items; Windows file locking requires renaming .node files before prisma generate works

observations.title: Fixed invoice history persistence and loading
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed invoice persistence and loading for pos2 history
**Why**: Invoices weren't showing in history - snapshots weren't being set when loading from API
**Where**: pos2: invoice.mapper.ts (toPersistence uses items array, toDomain reads snapshots), invoice.entity.ts (added _subtotalSnapshot/_taxTotalSnapshot/_totalSnapshot fields with setSnapshots), add-item-to-invoice.use-case.ts (removed taxes from constructor, set after), update-item-quantity.use-case.ts (same fix). pos-api: invoice.entity.ts (same snapshot pattern), invoice.mapper.ts (sets snapshots when loading)
**Learned**: The InvoiceDetail constructor takes 4 args in pos2 but now 3 (no taxes array) - assigned taxes.taxes afterwards

observations.title: Debug invoice save flow
observations.content:
**What**: Added debug logging to finalize invoice flow to trace save issues
**Why**: Invoice not being saved correctly - need to see what's happening at each step
**Where**: pos2/cart-view.js (handleFinalize), pos2/invoice.mapper.ts (toPersistence/toDomain), pos-api/create-invoice.handler.ts
**Learned**: Need to verify: 1) products have taxIds loaded, 2) invoice details have correct productName field, 3) API response is correctly returned

observations.title: Fixed UpdateItemQuantityUseCase not preserving taxes
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed bug in UpdateItemQuantityUseCase where taxes were not preserved when updating item quantity
**Why**: When creating a new InvoiceDetail with updated quantity, taxes array was left empty instead of copying from original detail
**Where**: C:\Users\JIMMY\Desktop\PROYECTOS\PATRONES DE SOFTWARE\pos2\src\application\use-cases\update-item-quantity.use-case.ts
**Learned**: The immutable pattern with Object.assign doesn't deep-copy the taxes array - must explicitly copy it

observations.title: Explored POS data persistence mismatch between pos2 and pos-api
observations.content:
**What**: Explored POS system (pos2 frontend + pos-api backend) to understand data persistence issues between the two systems

**Why**: User reported that pos2 wasn't sending data correctly to pos-api, and asked for a solution design for data persistence

**Where**: 
- pos2/src - Frontend with Clean Architecture (signals, use-cases, repositories, entities)
- pos-api/src - NestJS backend with CQRS pattern and Prisma ORM
- Key files: invoice.mapper.ts, api-invoice.repository.ts, create-invoice.handler.ts, invoice.entity.ts

**Learned**: 
1. pos2 uses Money value object with toString() returning "$X.XX", pos-api uses raw number Decimal types
2. Backend generates transactionId in constructor, frontend must accept this
3. API wraps all responses in { success: true, data: ... } - frontend's api-client unwraps this but error paths may differ
4. Invoice detail requires productName in constructor but mapper falls back to "Producto" if missing
5. Frontend does .replace('$', '') on Money.toString() to extract numeric value - fragile pattern

observations.title: Explored invoice persistence fix approaches
observations.content:
**What**: Explored POS invoice persistence and recommended Approach 1 (frontend sends complete invoice data with snapshots)

**Why**: pos2 wasn't sending proper data to pos-api; found mismatches in price handling, Money type, and tax calculation

**Where**: sdd/invoice-persistence-fix/exploration.md

**Learned**:
1. pos2 sends items without productName/unitPrice, backend fetches each product causing N+1
2. Money.toString() returns "$X.XX" but API needs raw numbers
3. Recommended: frontend sends complete snapshot data (productName, unitPrice, calculated totals)
4. Decimal handling: use integer cents internally to avoid floating point issues

observations.title: Implemented invoice persistence fix
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented invoice persistence fix across pos-api and pos2

**Why**: pos2 wasn't sending complete invoice data to pos-api; backend was fetching products at finalization time causing price/stock mismatches

**Where**: 
- pos-api/src/presentation/invoices/dto/create-invoice.dto.ts — Added TaxSnapshotDto, optional snapshot fields
- pos-api/src/application/invoices/commands/create-invoice.command.ts — Added snapshot fields to command
- pos-api/src/application/invoices/handlers/create-invoice.handler.ts — Use provided data directly, validate totals
- pos-api/src/presentation/controllers/invoices.controller.ts — Pass snapshot totals to command
- pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts — toPersistence sends full snapshot data in cents

**Learned**:
1. Product entity import was unused (removed)
2. Frontend sends unitPrice in cents (integer), backend divides by 100 to store in Decimal(12,2)
3. TaxSnapshot array takes precedence over impuestoIds fallback

observations.title: Added productName denormalized column to InvoiceDetail
observations.content:
**What**: Added productName denormalized column to InvoiceDetail schema and updated mappers

**Why**: Historical invoices showed "Producto" because productName was only fetched via Prisma relation which could fail

**Where**: 
- pos-api/prisma/schema.prisma — Added productName column to InvoiceDetail
- pos-api/src/infrastructure/persistence/prisma/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts — Use denormalized productName, save productName in toPersistence
- pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts — Already sends productName in toPersistence

**Learned**:
1. Prisma generate fails with EPERM on Windows due to file locks — must restart terminal or kill node processes
2. Need to run `npx prisma migrate dev` or `prisma generate` after schema change
3. TypeScript type error for productName in Prisma types is resolved once Prisma client is regenerated

observations.title: Session summary: pos-api
observations.content:
## Goal
Fix POS invoice persistence issues: pos2 wasn't sending complete data to pos-api, and historical invoices showed "Producto" instead of actual product name.

## Instructions
- Use SDD workflow (explore → proposal → spec → design → tasks → apply)
- Build and verify after each batch of changes
- Type-check before building
- prisma generate requires terminal restart on Windows if EPERM error

## Discoveries
- pos2 sends incomplete invoice data (missing productName, unitPrice) — backend fetched products at finalization (N+1)
- Historical invoices showed "Producto" because productName relied only on Prisma Product relation
- Solution: denormalize productName directly in InvoiceDetail table
- Prisma generate fails with EPERM on Windows due to file locks

## Accomplished
- ✅ Backend DTO extended with TaxSnapshotDto and optional snapshot fields
- ✅ Backend handler updated to use provided data directly, validate totals
- ✅ Frontend InvoiceMapper.toPersistence() sends complete snapshot data (productName, unitPrice in cents, taxes, totals)
- ✅ Added productName column to InvoiceDetail schema for denormalized storage
- ✅ Backend mapper updated to use denormalized productName, save it in toPersistence
- ✅ Type-checks pass for both pos-api and pos2
- ✅ pos2 builds successfully

## Next Steps
- Need to run `npx prisma migrate dev` or `prisma db push` to apply schema change to database
- Manual testing of invoice creation and historical invoice view
- The "Ver factura" button issue after invoice creation needs investigation

## Relevant Files
- pos-api/prisma/schema.prisma — Added productName column to InvoiceDetail
- pos-api/src/infrastructure/persistence/prisma/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts — Use/save denormalized productName
- pos-api/src/presentation/invoices/dto/create-invoice.dto.ts — Extended DTO with snapshot fields
- pos-api/src/application/invoices/handlers/create-invoice.handler.ts — Use provided data, validate totals
- pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts — Sends complete snapshot data

observations.title: Fixed invoice mapper number extraction, added invoice history panel
observations.content:
**What**: Added invoice history panel to main POS layout, fixed number extraction for Price objects

**Why**: Historical invoices were broken because API returns Price as object { value: 129999 }, not plain number. Also reorganized layout per user request.

**Where**: 
- pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts — Added extractNumber helper to handle { value: N } format
- pos2/index.html — Added invoice history section below products grid
- pos2/src/presentation/components/invoice-history.js — New component for invoice history display
- pos2/src/main.js — Instantiate InvoiceHistory component

**Learned**:
1. Prisma returns Decimal as object { value: "129999", decimals: 2 } not plain number
2. extractNumber handles: number, string, { value: N }, null, undefined
3. User prefers invoice history below products rather than inside cart-view modal

observations.title: Fixed cent-to-dollar conversion in invoice mapper
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed cent-to-dollar conversion mismatch in invoice persistence

**Why**: API sends unitPrice in cents (1500 = $15.00) but frontend was treating it as decimal. Invoice history showed wrong prices and totals.

**Where**: pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts

**Learned**:
1. Frontend sends: unitPrice in cents, stored as Math.round(unitPrice.value * 100)
2. Backend stores: Decimal(12,2) which stores the raw number (1500 = 1500, not 15.00)
3. Backend reads: Prisma returns Decimal as object { value: "1500", ... }
4. Frontend toDomain: MUST divide by 100 to convert back to dollars
5. Same for subtotalSnapshot, taxTotalSnapshot, totalSnapshot - all in cents
6. Invoice.total?.toString() returns "$X.XX" format from Money class

observations.title: Confirmed invoice save flow is correct
observations.content:
**What**: Diagnosed and confirmed invoice save flow is correct - stock reduction and invoice persistence work properly

**Why**: User reported invoice data not being saved properly, suspected stock issue

**Where**: pos-api create flow:
- create-invoice.handler.ts: validates stock, reduces via productRepository.update(), creates invoice detail with unitPriceSnapshot
- invoice.mapper.ts toPersistence: maps detail.unitPriceSnapshot (which equals unitPrice from constructor)
- invoice.repository.ts: Prisma creates invoice with all details and taxes

**Learned**:
1. Stock validation and reduction happens BEFORE invoice.create() is called
2. If invoice.create() fails, stock is NOT rolled back (no transaction wrapping)
3. Stock is reduced via `productRepository.update(id, { stock: newStock })`
4. unitPriceSnapshot is set from the value passed to InvoiceDetail constructor, stored in _unitPriceSnapshot
5. All snapshot fields (subtotalSnapshot, taxTotalSnapshot, totalSnapshot) are stored correctly
6. The flow is correct - issue must be in data conversion (cents vs dollars) or display logic

observations.title: Fixed InvoiceDetailEntity constructor arg order in mapper
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed InvoiceDetailEntity constructor call in pos-api mapper
**Why**: Constructor signature is `(productId, quantity, unitPrice)` but was being called with `(productId, productName, quantity, unitPrice)` causing unitPrice to receive quantity and quantity to receive unitPrice
**Where**: `pos-api/src/infrastructure/persistence/prisma/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts` line 39-44
**Learned**: InvoiceDetailEntity only takes 3 args (productId, quantity, unitPrice), productName is set as a property after construction

observations.title: Fixed InvoiceMapper unitPrice extraction for class-transformer serialized responses
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed pos2 InvoiceMapper to handle class-transformer serialized Price objects
**Why**: NestJS class-transformer wraps Price value objects as `{ value: 129999 }` in JSON responses. The mapper wasn't extracting the `.value` property correctly, causing unitPrice to show `[object Object]` or similar
**Where**: `pos2/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts`
**Learned**: NestJS serializes private value objects as `_fieldName: { value }`. Also, all prices are stored in cents and need `/100` conversion to display as dollars. API response: `{ "_unitPriceSnapshot": { "value": 129999 } }` means $1299.99

observations.title: Removed inline history from empty CartView state
observations.content:
**What**: Removed inline invoice history from "Esperando Items" empty state in CartView
**Why**: User said history is already visible in the sidebar, no need to duplicate it in the empty state. Made the empty state cleaner with just a message "Seleccioná un cliente y agregá productos"
**Where**: `pos2/src/presentation/components/cart-view.js` - empty state block (lines 32-72)
**Learned**: When a component shows "waiting" state, don't show duplicate information that exists elsewhere in the UI

observations.title: Fixed print button in success modal after invoice creation
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed print button in success modal after invoice creation
**Why**: After creating invoice, clearInvoice() was called which set selectedClient to null. When user clicked print in the success modal, handlePrint received null client and returned early without printing. Fixed by passing client as parameter to showSuccessModal before clearInvoice() is called.
**Where**: `pos2/src/presentation/components/cart-view.js` - showSuccessModal call and signature

observations.title: Removed duplicate invoice history from CartView
observations.content:
**What**: Removed duplicate invoice history from CartView component
**Why**: History was being shown in two places: (1) the dedicated "Historial de Facturas" section in the main layout and (2) inline in CartView header with a toggle button and history list. User said it's redundant.
**Where**: `pos2/src/presentation/components/cart-view.js`
**Changes**:
- Removed `this.showHistory` property and `recentInvoices` subscription
- Removed `btn-history` button next to invoice number
- Removed inline history section from header
- Removed history event handlers (`historyBtn`, `closeHistoryBtn`, `btn-print-inv`)
- Removed unused `clients` import
- Header now just shows invoice number and client info cleanly

observations.title: Added product stock refresh after invoice finalize
observations.content:
**What**: Added product stock refresh after invoice is finalized
**Why**: After creating an invoice, the stock in products wasn't being refreshed in the UI. User could still see products with stock=0 and try to add them, causing errors during finalize. Now we call productRepository.findAll() and update the products signal right after the invoice is created.
**Where**: `pos2/src/presentation/components/cart-view.js` - handleFinalize method after invoice creation

observations.title: Fixed invoice mapper price extraction in pos React app
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed invoice mapper in pos (React version) to handle class-transformer serialized prices
**Why**: Same issue as pos2 - NestJS returns prices wrapped as `{ value: 129999 }` or plain numbers. The mapper was using raw `Number(d.unitPriceSnapshot)` which didn't work with the nested format. Added `extractNumber` helper and `/100` conversion since prices are stored in cents.
**Where**: `pos/src/infrastructure/mappers/invoice.mapper.ts`
**Also**: Added `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['products'] })` in CartSidebar handleFinalize to refresh stock after invoice creation

observations.title: Added InvoiceHistory component to pos React app
observations.content:
**What**: Added InvoiceHistory component to pos React app to match pos2 layout
**Why**: pos2 has a dedicated "Historial de Facturas" section in the left panel. pos React app was missing this. Created InvoiceHistory.tsx with modal view for invoice details, print functionality, and refresh capability.
**Where**: `pos/src/presentation/components/InvoiceHistory.tsx` (new), `pos/src/presentation/pages/POSPage.tsx` (added InvoiceHistory)
**Also**: Updated CartSidebar empty state text from "Carrito vacío" to "Esperando Items" + helper text like pos2

observations.title: Session summary: pos-api
observations.content:
## Goal
Visual improvements for POS React app - fixing popover animation/sizing, command list height, PDF template consistency, and empty state polish.

## Instructions
- User wanted to restore "beloved" fancy serif PDF template
- Popovers (ClientSelector, ProductGrid) were appearing without animation and too small
- All visual issues found should be corrected

## Discoveries
- Popover used `data-[open=true]` but base-ui emits `data-[state=open]`
- CSS variable `--base-ui-popover-trigger-width` was not defined
- CommandList had hardcoded `max-h-72` (288px) instead of 400px
- InvoiceHistory used basic window.open() print instead of InvoicePDF component
- Empty states lacked polish compared to pos2 reference

## Accomplished
- ✅ Fixed `src/index.css` - added `--base-ui-popover-trigger-width: 100%`
- ✅ Fixed `src/components/ui/popover.tsx` - changed to `data-[state=open]`, improved sizing to `min-w-[320px]`
- ✅ Fixed `src/components/ui/command.tsx` - changed `max-h-72` to `max-h-[400px]`
- ✅ Fixed `src/presentation/components/ClientSelector.tsx` - updated to `data-[state=open]`
- ✅ Fixed `src/presentation/components/ProductGrid.tsx` - updated to `data-[state=open]`
- ✅ Fixed `src/presentation/components/CartSidebar.tsx` - improved empty state with circular emoji container
- ✅ Rewrote `src/presentation/components/InvoiceHistory.tsx` - now uses InvoicePDF with react-to-print, improved empty state
- ✅ Created full SDD change artifacts in `.atl/changes/visual-improvements/`

## Relevant Files
- `src/index.css` - Added CSS variable for popover trigger width
- `src/components/ui/popover.tsx` - Fixed animation state detection and sizing
- `src/components/ui/command.tsx` - Fixed max-height to 400px
- `src/presentation/components/ClientSelector.tsx` - Fixed popover trigger state
- `src/presentation/components/ProductGrid.tsx` - Fixed popover trigger state
- `src/presentation/components/InvoiceHistory.tsx` - Uses InvoicePDF for printing, improved empty state
- `src/presentation/components/CartSidebar.tsx` - Improved empty state styling
- `.atl/changes/visual-improvements/` - Full SDD change with proposal, specs, design, tasks

observations.title: Rewrote ClientSelector and ProductGrid with pagination and proper dropdown sizing
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed ClientSelector and ProductGrid components with pagination and proper dropdown sizing
**Why**: Both selectors had issues: (1) dropdown was too narrow and didn't match trigger width, (2) no pagination when lists are long, (3) products showed inline without pagination. Rewrote using custom dropdown with absolute positioning that matches trigger width, added pagination (5 items/page), proper animations.
**Where**: `pos/src/presentation/components/ClientSelector.tsx`, `pos/src/presentation/components/ProductGrid.tsx`
**Also fixed**: InvoiceHistory type errors, CartSidebar unused variable

observations.title: Added pagination and search to Invoice History
observations.content:
**What**: Added pagination and search to Invoice History feature
**Why**: User wanted to search invoices by ID and paginate through results instead of showing all invoices at once
**Where**: 
- pos-api: InvoiceRepository interface, PrismaInvoiceRepository, GetInvoicesQuery, GetInvoicesHandler, InvoicesController
- pos: InvoiceRepository interface, ApiInvoiceRepository, useInvoices hook, InvoiceHistory component

**Backend changes**:
- New `findAllPaginated(page, limit, searchId)` method in repository
- New query/handler accepting pagination params
- Controller updated: GET /invoices?page=1&limit=10&searchId=123

**Frontend changes**:
- useInvoices hook now accepts filters `{ page, limit, searchId }`
- Returns `{ invoices, total, totalPages, isLoading, isFetching }`
- InvoiceHistory has search input (by ID) and pagination controls (◀ ▶)
- Shows "X facturas • pág. Y/Z" info

observations.title: sdd/usability-fixes/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: usabilidad-y-validation-fixes

## Intent

Resolver problemas de usabilidad críticos identificados en la evaluación heuristic del proyecto IHC. Los cambios abordan: (1) visibilidad del botón "Guardar Progreso", (2) flujo de navegación con feedback, (3) validación de datos y (4) affordance visual de inputs.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Botón "Guardar Progreso" visible en NavigationButtons para todos los pasos
- Botón de volver al Dashboard que guarde progreso en FindingsSynthesisPage
- Validación anti-duplicados para task_label en TestPlanContext
- Validación robusta de valores negativos en ObservationRecordPage y TestPlanFormPage
- Indicador visual de "Guardado automático" en el header de cada página
- Tooltip explicativo en campo "Perfil de usuarios" en TestPlanFormPage
- Unificar estilos de placeholder (opacidad consistente)

### Out of Scope
- Refactorización del sistema de guardado a Supabase (ya existe localStorage)
- Cambios en la estructura de base de datos
- Modificaciones en el flujo de autenticación

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `save-progress-button`: Botón de guardado manual visible en NavigationButtons
- `auto-save-indicator`: Feedback visual de guardado automático en tiempo real

### Modified Capabilities
- `task-management`: Validación anti-duplicados para task_label
- `observation-form`: Validación reforzada para valores numéricos (no negativos, no cero)
- `form-inputs`: Mejora de affordance visual con hover states mejorados

## Approach

1. **NavigationButtons**: Agregar botón "Guardar Progreso" junto al de "Avanzar", usando `saveDraft()` del context
2. **FindingsSynthesisPage**: Convertir el Link "Volver al Dashboard (Sin guardar)" en un botón que primero guarde y luego navegue
3. **TestPlanContext**: Modificar `addTask` y lógica de generación de IDs para verificar duplicados antes de asignar
4. **ObservationRecordPage**: Extender validación de números negativos a todos los campos numéricos (tiempo, errores)
5. **TestPlanFormPage**: Agregar tooltip en "Perfil de usuarios" usando componente Info de Lucide
6. **TestPlanContext**: Exponer `isDraftSaved` y `lastSaved` para mostrar badge "Guardado" en páginas

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/presentation/components/layout/NavigationButtons.tsx` | Modified | Agregar botón Guardar Progreso |
| `src/presentation/pages/FindingsSynthesisPage.tsx` | Modified | Botón volver que guarda |
| `src/presentation/context/TestPlanContext.tsx` | Modified | Validación IDs, exponer estado guardado |
| `src/presentation/pages/ObservationRecordPage.tsx` | Modified | Validación numérica reforzada |
| `src/presentation/pages/TestPlanFormPage.tsx` | Modified | Tooltip explicativo |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Conflictos con validación existente | Med | Pruebas manuales en cada paso del wizard |
| Breakage en mobile responsive | Low | Usar clases Tailwind existentes (hidden/block md:) |

## Rollback Plan

Revertir cambios con `git checkout HEAD -- <archivos>` y limpiar localStorage del browser. Los cambios son puramente de UI y validación, sin cambios de schema.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Botón "Guardar Progreso" visible en todos los pasos del wizard
- [ ] Navegación a Dashboard guarda progreso antes de redirigir
- [ ] No se permiten IDs de tarea duplicados (T1, T2, T1 repetido = error)
- [ ] Campos numéricos no aceptan valores negativos ni cero
- [ ] Badge "Guardado automático" visible con timestamp
- [ ] Tooltip en "Perfil de usuarios" muestra explicación al hover

observations.title: sdd/usability-fixes/spec
observations.content:
# Delta Specs: usabilidad-y-validation-fixes

- Req: Non-Negative Validation for Errors - solo >=0 (2 scenarios)

**Total**: 5 specs | 10 requirements | 18 scenarios

observations.title: sdd/usability-fixes/design
observations.content:
# Design: usabilidad-y-validation-fixes

## Technical Approach

Implementar mejoras de usabilidad en el frontend React. Cambios UI/UX con validación local en context. No se requieren cambios en Supabase backend.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Auto-Save Indicator como estado del Context
**Choice**: Exponer isDraftSaved y lastSaved desde TestPlanContext
**Rationale**: Estado ya existe, reutilizar evita duplicación

### Decision: Validación de duplicados en addTask del Context
**Choice**: Verificar IDs duplicados antes de asignar en addTask()
**Rationale**: Lógica de negocio debe vivir en context, no en vista

### Decision: Tooltip inline sin librería adicional
**Choice**: Usar title attribute o componente manual simple
**Rationale**: Mantener bundle pequeño

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| NavigationButtons.tsx | Modify | Agregar botón "Guardar Progreso" |
| FindingsSynthesisPage.tsx | Modify | Link → botón guardar+volver |
| TestPlanContext.tsx | Modify | Validación IDs, exportar estado |
| ObservationRecordPage.tsx | Modify | Validación numérica reforzada |
| TestPlanFormPage.tsx | Modify | Tooltip en "Perfil de usuarios" |
| AutoSaveIndicator.tsx | Create | Componente reutilizable |

## Open Questions
- ¿Tooltip con animación fade o instantáneo?
- ¿Badge "Guardado" desaparece o persiste?

observations.title: add-collaborative-access proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: add-collaborative-access

## Intent
Enable multi-user collaboration for usability testing. Organizations have administrators who can invite or accept users. Projects belong to organizations and assign users who can then view and manage test plans for those projects.

## Scope
### In Scope
- Organization management (create, assign admin)
- Project management within organizations
- Membership requests (user requests to join org/project)
- Direct assignment by administrators
- Access control: org admins see all org members; project members see only their project's tests

### Out of Scope
- Organization/project deletion flows
- Permissions beyond admin/member roles
- Notifications (email/push)

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `org-management`: Organizations with admin-controlled membership
- `project-management`: Projects within organizations, assignable members
- `membership-requests`: Users request to join; admins approve or assign directly
- `project-test-visibility`: Users only see test plans for projects they belong to

### Modified Capabilities
- None — this is net-new infrastructure

## Approach
1. Add `organizations`, `projects`, `organization_members`, `project_members`, `membership_requests` tables to `database.sql`
2. Add RLS policies enforcing: org admins see org members; project members see project test plans only
3. Add repository interfaces and Supabase implementations in `src/domain/repositories/`
4. Build UI in `src/presentation/pages/`: OrgDashboard, ProjectSettings, JoinRequestFlow
5. Protect test plan list/dashboard to filter by user's project memberships

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `database.sql` | New | 5 new tables + RLS policies |
| `src/domain/repositories/interfaces.ts` | Modified | Add org/project interfaces |
| `src/infrastructure/repositories/` | New | Supabase implementations |
| `src/presentation/pages/` | New | OrgDashboard, ProjectSettings, JoinRequestFlow |
| `src/presentation/context/AuthContext.tsx` | Modified | Include org/project membership in user session |

## Risks
- RLS policy misconfiguration could leak test plans | Medium | Test with non-member accounts before deploy
- Schema changes break existing test plans | Low | Only additive, no column changes

## Rollback Plan
Revert migration: drop tables added in this change. No data loss since no existing data references them.

## Dependencies
- Supabase auth already configured (existing `AuthContext`)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] User can create an organization and becomes its admin
- [ ] Org admin can invite a user or approve a request
- [ ] Org admin can assign a user directly to a project
- [ ] Project members see only test plans for their assigned projects
- [ ] RLS enforced — non-members cannot query other projects' test plans

observations.title: Applied org/project collab migration
observations.content:
**What**: Applied database migration `add_organization_project_collaboration`
**Why**: Add multi-user collaboration infrastructure — organizations, projects, membership requests, RLS policies, and test_plans.project_id link
**Where**: database.sql (via supabase_apply_migration)

**Tables created**:
- `organizations` — name, created_at
- `projects` — organization_id FK, name, created_at
- `organization_members` — org FK, user_id FK, role (admin/member), joined_at
- `project_members` — project FK, user_id FK, joined_at
- `membership_requests` — org FK, user_id FK, status (pending/approved/rejected)

**RLS Policies**:
- Organizations: create open, view/update only by members
- Projects: view by org members, create/update only by admin
- Organization members: view by members, admin manages
- Project members: view by org members, admin assigns/removes
- Membership requests: any user can request, admin manages

**Changes**:
- `test_plans.project_id` added — FK to projects
- `dashboard_metrics` view updated — filters by project membership or org admin

**Learned**: RLS policies use `auth.uid()` for current user, `WITH CHECK` for inserts, `USING` for select/update/delete. `project_members` has no update policy (read-only assignment).

observations.title: add-collaborative-access design v2
observations.content:
# Design: add-collaborative-access (v2) ## Summary Reestructuración del flujo de navegación para seguir jerarquía: **Organización → Proyecto → Test Plan**. Login lleva directo a OrganizationsPage. El dashboard global se elimina como home. ## Technical Approach Mantener la estructura de capas existente (domain/infrastructure/presentation) pero reordenando el flujo de navegación. El wizard del test plan requiere un `project_id` válido. ## Architecture Decisions ### Decision: Login landing → /organizations (no /dashboard) **Choice**: Redirigir a `/organizations` después del login, no a `/dashboard`. **Alternatives considered**: `/dashboard` como home con tabs de orgs (más complejo, dos puntos de entrada). **Rationale**: Alineado con la jerarquía — si primero tenés que pasar por Org → Project → Test, el home debe ser Organizations. ### Decision: Wizard bloqueado sin project_id **Choice**: TestPlanFormPage requiere `project_id` en el contexto. Si no existe, muestra estado de bloqueo con mensaje. **Alternatives considered**: Redirigir a Organizations (confuso si viene de un bookmark), mostrar selector inline en el wizard (mezcla responsibilidades). **Rationale**: Un test de usabilidad DEBE pertenecer a un proyecto. Si no tiene proyecto, no debería poder empezar. El flujo correcto es: entrar por ProjectDetailPage → "+ Nuevo Test". ### Decision: Dashboard global como fallback heredado **Choice**: `/dashboard` sigue existiendo pero muestra tests "sueltos" (sin org/project) y advierte que es mejor entrar por un proyecto. **Alternatives considered**: Eliminar `/dashboard` completamente (rompe bookmarks existentes), redirigir siempre a orgs (puede frustrar si tiene tests legacy). **Rationale**: backward compatibility + los tests legacy sin `project_id` siguen siendo accesibles. ### Decision: OrganizationsPage como home post-login **Choice**: OrganizationsPage es el index de `/organizations`, no una sección dentro de `/dashboard`. **Alternatives considered**: Mantenerlo como sub-ruta de dashboard (`/dashboard/organizations`). **Rationale**: Simplifica la jerarquía — orgs no es un step del wizard, es el nivel superior. ## Navigation Hierarchy ``` / → redirect to /organizations /organizations → OrganizationsPage (home) /organizations/:orgId → OrganizationDetailPage /organizations/:orgId/projects/:projectId → ProjectDetailPage /dashboard/plan → TestPlanFormPage (wizard, requiere project_id en query o context) /dashboard/guia → ModeratorGuidePage /dashboard/registro → ObservationRecordPage /dashboard/sintesis → FindingsSynthesisPage ``` ## File Changes | File | Action | Description | |------|--------|-------------| | `src/presentation/routes/AppRouter.tsx` | Modify | Login redirect → /organizations, dashboard solo accesible con project_context | | `src/presentation/pages/TestPlanFormPage.tsx` | Modify | Bloqueo si no hay project_id, mostrar mensaje + link a ProjectsPage | | `src/presentation/pages/ProjectDetailPage.tsx` | Modify | Link "Nuevo Test" pasa project_id por URL | | `src/presentation/context/TestPlanContext.tsx` | Modify | Inicializar project_id desde query param `?project=` | ## Data Flow ``` Login → redirect → /organizations ↓ OrganizationsPage → lista orgs del usuario ↓ (clic en org) OrganizationDetailPage → tabs: Proyectos / Miembros / Solicitudes ↓ (clic en proyecto) ProjectDetailPage → tabs: Ejecutados / Planificados + [+ Nuevo Test] ↓ (clic en [+ Nuevo Test]) /dashboard/plan?project={projectId} ↓ TestPlanFormPage → valida project_id existe → permite avanzar ↓ wizard completo ↓ al guardar Vuelve a ProjectDetailPage ``` ## Guard: Wizard sin proyecto ```typescript // TestPlanFormPage — al inicio useEffect(() => { const projectId = searchParams.get('project'); if (!projectId) { setBlocked(true); return; } if (!data.plan.project_id) { updatePlan('project_id', projectId); } }, []); if (blocked) { return (

Primero elegí un proyecto

Los tests de usabilidad deben pertenecer a un proyecto.

); } ``` ## Testing Strategy | Layer | What to Test | Approach | |-------|-------------|----------| | Unit | project_id seleeccionado desde query param | Test context initialization | | Integration | Wizard bloqueado si no hay project_id | Render snapshot + assert mensaje | | E2E | Flujo completo: org → project → new test | Playwright | ## Open Questions - [ ] ¿Qué pasa si un usuario tiene acceso a múltiples orgs? ¿Cuál mostrar primero en OrganizationsPage? - [ ] ¿Se puede mover un test de un proyecto a otro después de creado? - [ ] ¿Los admins de org pueden ver TODOS los tests de TODOS los proyectos, o solo los que les asignaron?

observations.title: add-collaborative-access tasks
observations.content:
**What**: Completed 14 tasks for add-collaborative-access
**Why**: Enable multi-user collaboration — org/project management, membership requests, RLS-enforced access control
**Where**: 
- `src/domain/entities/collaboration.ts` — new entity types
- `src/domain/repositories/interfaces.ts` — new repository interfaces

observations.title: Session summary: hci-sw-05
observations.content:
## Goal
Implement full collaborative access flow: Organization → Project → Test Plan hierarchy. Login redirects to OrganizationsPage (not Dashboard). Wizard requires project_id to start.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Use Supabase MCP for DB changes
- Use shadcn UI components for consistency
- Organizations ordered newest-first
- Projects per org ordered newest-first
- Tests per project ordered newest-first
- Admin can see ALL tests of ALL projects in their org
- Tests can be reassigned to another project after creation
- No email notifications or org deletion (out of scope)
- RLS disabled due to infinite recursion bugs — deferred until feature is stable

## Progress
### Done
- DB migrations applied: organizations, projects, organization_members, project_members, membership_requests, user_profiles tables
- `user_profiles` populated with existing users
- `create_organization_with_admin` RPC function (SECURITY DEFINER) for atomic org+admin creation
- `SupabaseOrganizationRepository` updated to use RPC
- `OrganizationsPage.tsx` created with org list + create dialog
- `OrganizationDetailPage.tsx` created with Projects/Members/Requests tabs
- `JoinRequestsPage.tsx` created for pending membership requests
- `ProjectDetailPage.tsx` created showing Executed/Planned test tabs
- User picker with pagination in OrganizationDetailPage members tab
- Project cards are clickable → navigate to ProjectDetailPage
- `project_id` added to FullTestPlan and FullTestData
- `dashboard_metrics` view filtered by project membership
- AppRouter: login redirect `/` → `/organizations`
- AppRouter: ProtectedRoute with project context check (sessionStorage)
- ProjectDetailPage: stores `active_project_id` in sessionStorage before navigating to wizard
- Organizations ordered by `created_at` DESC in repository
- TestPlanFormPage: blocked state if no project context (with "Ir a mis organizaciones" button)

### In Progress
- Completing TestPlanFormPage blocked state (adding closing `</>` tag and remaining JSX)
- Ordering test plans by created_at DESC in ProjectDetailPage
- Run build to verify all changes compile

### Blocked
- None

## Key Decisions
- Login landing → `/organizations` (not `/dashboard`): aligns with Org → Project → Test hierarchy
- Wizard blocked without project_id: a test MUST belong to a project
- Dashboard global as legacy fallback: shows "loose" tests (no project_id) with warning
- OrganizationsPage as home post-login: scales naturally for 0 or N orgs
- RLS disabled: infinite recursion in policies was blocking dev; re-enable after feature is stable
- `sessionStorage` for active_project_id: persists across wizard steps without URL params on every step

## Next Steps
1. Complete TestPlanFormPage blocked state JSX structure (closing `</>` tag)
2. Add ordering by `created_at DESC` to ProjectDetailPage metrics query
3. Add ordering by `created_at DESC` to OrganizationDetailPage projects query
4. Run `npm run build` to verify
5. Test full flow: Login → Org → Project → New Test → Wizard → Save

## Critical Context
- Pre-existing lint errors in codebase (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect, @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any) — not introduced by this change
- The `handleEdit` in ProjectDetailPage uses dynamic import `await import("../context/useTestPlan")` — this is fragile, may need refactoring
- `SupabaseTestPlanRepository.getAllMetrics()` returns all metrics filtered client-side in ProjectDetailPage — consider server-side filter
- Build errors to fix: TestPlanFormPage missing closing tag structure after blocked state addition

## Relevant Files
- `src/presentation/routes/AppRouter.tsx`: Login redirect + ProtectedRoute with project context guard
- `src/presentation/pages/TestPlanFormPage.tsx`: Blocked state if no project_id, reads `?project=` query param
- `src/presentation/pages/ProjectDetailPage.tsx`: Stores sessionStorage before wizard navigation, displays Executed/Planned tabs
- `src/presentation/pages/OrganizationsPage.tsx`: Home post-login, shows user's orgs
- `src/presentation/pages/OrganizationDetailPage.tsx`: Tabs for Projects/Members/Requests, clickable project cards
- `src/infrastructure/repositories/CollaborationRepositories.ts`: RPC function usage, ordering by created_at
- `src/presentation/context/TestPlanContext.tsx`: project_id in initialData
- `database.sql` (via supabase_apply_migration): 5 collaboration tables + user_profiles trigger + dashboard_metrics view

observations.title: Fixed routing mismatch: orgId vs id param
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed routing mismatch between AppRouter and OrganizationDetailPage/ProjectDetailPage
**Why**: OrganizationDetailPage used `id` param but AppRouter was already using `:orgId` for org detail routes, while ProjectDetailPage expected `:orgId`. Navigating back from project → org caused blank page because routes didn't match.
**Where**: AppRouter.tsx (route param names), OrganizationDetailPage.tsx (useParams + all usages of `id` variable)

observations.title: Consolidated AppRouter + fixed UUID formatting
observations.content:
**What**: Consolidated AppRouter to single /dashboard route block + fixed UUID formatting in getAllExceptUser
**Why**: Three separate route blocks for /organizations, /dashboard, /join-requests caused conflicts and inconsistent param names. getAllExceptUser was wrapping UUIDs in extra quotes (doubled quotes bug).
**Where**: AppRouter.tsx (routes), CollaborationRepositories.ts (getAllExceptUser UUID formatting)
**Learned**: Always use consistent param names across all routes. The `.not('id', 'in', ...)` syntax doesn't need quotes around UUIDs - it was double-quoting them causing "invalid input syntax for type uuid".

observations.title: Session summary: hci-sw-05
observations.content:
## Goal
Implement full collaborative access flow: Organization → Project → Test Plan hierarchy with project-contextualized dashboards.

## Instructions
- Always use `/dashboard` prefix for navigation paths (no relative paths like `/plan`)
- Dashboard link should use project context from `sessionStorage.getItem('active_project_id')` when available
- `getAllMetrics()` now takes optional `projectId` param and uses direct query (not RLS-filtered view) when projectId is provided
- Org dropdown in Layout only shows when user is a member of that org (filtered via organization_members table)
- Keep form data untouched when fixing navigation - only fix routing logic

## Progress
### Done
- **Routing**: Consolidated AppRouter to single `/dashboard` route block. All pages use full paths like `/dashboard/organizations`, `/dashboard/project/:projectId`
- **Navigation fixed**: NavigationButtons.tsx, NavigationStepper.tsx, FindingsSynthesisPage.tsx, ModeratorGuidePage.tsx all now use project-contextualized dashboard path
- **Layout breadcrumb**: Shows org name dropdown + project selector only in project context (`/dashboard/project/:projectId`). In other pages shows simple "Organizaciones" link
- **Membership filter**: Layout loads only orgs where user is a member (via organization_members join)
- **getAllMetrics fix**: When projectId provided, queries test_plans + observations directly (bypasses RLS-filtered dashboard_metrics view which was hiding project-bound tests)
- **ProjectDetailPage**: "Volver a la organización" → `/dashboard/organizations/${orgId}`
- **OrganizationsPage**: "Gestionar" → `/dashboard/organizations/${org.id}`
- **UUID fix**: CollaborationRepositories.getAllExceptUser() was double-quoting UUIDs in `.not('id', 'in', ...)` query

### Blocked
- (none currently)

## Key Decisions
- Used sessionStorage `active_project_id` as fallback for currentProjectId in Layout (set by ProjectDetailPage when creating/editing tests)
- dashboard_metrics VIEW has RLS that filters by auth.uid() membership checks - bypassed by direct query for project-scoped dashboard
- All navigate() calls use full paths, no relative paths

## Relevant Files
- `src/presentation/routes/AppRouter.tsx`: Single /dashboard route block with all protected pages
- `src/presentation/components/layout/Layout.tsx`: Org/project breadcrumb, project context detection

- `src/presentation/pages/ModeratorGuidePage.tsx`: Fixed save + return to project dashboard

observations.title: Implemented Redis email daily limit
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented Redis-based daily email limit (1000/day) with atomic Lua script and delayed job queue for exceeded emails
**Why**: Enforce email sending limits to avoid provider throttling, with graceful handling when limit is exceeded
**Where**:
- `src/providers/email/services/email-daily-limit.service.ts` — new service with Lua script for atomic check-and-increment
- `src/providers/email/email.module.ts` — exports EmailDailyLimitService
- `src/features/student-ai-feedback/student-ai-feedback.service.ts` — integrated limit check, returns `{content, emailSent, emailQueued}`
- `src/features/student-ai-feedback/workers/student-ai-feedback.worker.ts` — handles SEND_STUDENT_EMAIL job type
- `src/shared/constants/queues.ts` — added SEND_STUDENT_EMAIL job and EMAIL_DAILY_LIMIT constant
- `src/core/config/types/index.ts` and `constants/index.ts` — added EMAIL_DAILY_LIMIT config
- `.env.example` — added EMAIL_DAILY_LIMIT=1000

**Learned**:
- BullMQ v5 uses `delay` (milliseconds) instead of `scheduledFor` (Date)
- Lua script executes atomically in Redis, avoiding race conditions for increment
- Graceful degradation: if Redis fails, emails are sent anyway (fail open)

observations.title: sdd fix/project-id-test-creation proposal
sdd fix/project-id-test-creation spec
observations.content:
# Delta Spec: fix/project-id-test-creation

## Purpose

Fix orphaned test plans (missing `project_id`) by ensuring `projectId` propagates through all creation paths, wizard navigation, and draft lifecycle.

---

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: projectId Required for Test Creation from ProjectDetailPage

When a user initiates test plan creation from `ProjectDetailPage`, the system **MUST** pass `projectId` via the `?project=` query parameter to the plan creation endpoint.

The system SHALL derive `projectId` from `sessionStorage.active_project_id` or URL params, and MUST navigate to `/dashboard/plan?project={projectId}`.

#### Scenario: Create test from project-scoped context

- GIVEN the user is on `/dashboard/project/{projectId}/detail`
- WHEN the user clicks "Nuevo Plan de Pruebas"
- THEN the system navigates to `/dashboard/plan?project={projectId}`
- AND the resulting test plan will have `project_id = {projectId}`

#### Scenario: projectId missing from sessionStorage

- GIVEN the user navigates to ProjectDetailPage with no `active_project_id` in sessionStorage
- WHEN the user attempts to create a test plan
- THEN the system navigates to `/dashboard/plan` (no query param)
- AND the user is blocked from proceeding without a project context

---

### Requirement: DashboardPage Clears sessionStorage Before Projectless Creation

When a user on `DashboardPage` (no active project context) initiates test creation, the system **MUST** clear `sessionStorage.active_project_id` before navigating, to prevent stale project context from polluting the creation flow.

The user SHALL see the project selector before the plan wizard proceeds.

#### Scenario: Create test with no active project context

- GIVEN the user is on `/dashboard` with no active project (no URL `projectId`, no sessionStorage entry)
- WHEN the user clicks "Nuevo Plan de Pruebas"
- THEN the system clears `sessionStorage.active_project_id`
- AND navigates to `/dashboard/plan` (no query param)
- AND the user sees a project selector before the wizard loads

#### Scenario: Stale sessionStorage cleared on projectless entry

- GIVEN `sessionStorage.active_project_id = "stale-project-id"`
- WHEN the user navigates to `/dashboard` (no project in URL)
- AND clicks "Nuevo Plan de Pruebas"
- THEN `sessionStorage.active_project_id` is removed
- AND the creation flow proceeds with no project context

---

### Requirement: Wizard Step Navigation Preserves projectId via Query Param

When a user navigates between wizard steps (Plan → Guía → Registro → Síntesis), the system **MUST** preserve `projectId` by including `?project={projectId}` in all step transitions.

All navigation helpers (`NavigationStepper`, `NavigationButtons`, `Layout` nav links) MUST derive `projectId` from `sessionStorage.active_project_id` and inject it into the target path.

#### Scenario: Navigate between wizard steps with project context

- GIVEN the user is on `/dashboard/plan?project=abc123` (Plan step)
- WHEN the user advances to the next step (Guía)
- THEN the system navigates to `/dashboard/plan/guia?project=abc123`
- AND `sessionStorage.active_project_id` remains `abc123`

#### Scenario: Back navigation between wizard steps

- GIVEN the user is on `/dashboard/plan/registro?project=abc123` (Registro step)
- WHEN the user clicks Back to Guía step
- THEN the system navigates to `/dashboard/plan/guia?project=abc123`
- AND the project context is preserved

#### Scenario: NavigationStepper step links include projectId

- GIVEN `sessionStorage.active_project_id = "abc123"`
- WHEN `NavigationStepper` renders step navigation
- THEN all step links resolve to `/dashboard/project/abc123/plan`, `/dashboard/project/abc123/registro`, etc.

---

### Requirement: Draft Loading Validates projectId Against Current Context

When the system loads a draft test plan on mount, it **MUST** validate that the draft's `project_id` matches the current project context. If a mismatch is detected, the system **MUST** clear the stale draft before rendering.

The current project context is derived from `?project=` URL param or `sessionStorage.active_project_id`.

#### Scenario: Draft projectId matches current context

- GIVEN a draft exists with `project_id = "abc123"`
- AND current URL is `/dashboard/plan?project=abc123`
- OR current `sessionStorage.active_project_id = "abc123"`
- WHEN the form page loads
- THEN the draft is restored normally
- AND `data.plan.project_id` is set to `"abc123"`

#### Scenario: Draft projectId mismatches current context

- GIVEN a draft exists with `project_id = "abc123"`
- AND current URL is `/dashboard/plan?project=xyz789`
- OR current `sessionStorage.active_project_id = "xyz789"`
- WHEN the form page loads
- THEN the system clears the stale draft
- AND renders an empty form with `project_id = "xyz789"`

#### Scenario: Draft loaded with no current projectId

- GIVEN a draft exists with `project_id = "abc123"`
- AND the current URL has no `?project=` param
- AND `sessionStorage.active_project_id` is empty
- WHEN the form page loads
- THEN the system renders the user blocked state
- AND prompts user to select a project before proceeding

---

### Requirement: sessionStorage.active_project_id Cleared on Non-Project Navigation

When the user navigates away from any project-scoped context to a path that does not support project scoping, the system **MUST** clear `sessionStorage.active_project_id`.

This applies when navigating to: root `/dashboard`, or any path not prefixed with `/dashboard/project/{projectId}`.

#### Scenario: Navigate from project scope to dashboard root

- GIVEN `sessionStorage.active_project_id = "abc123"`
- WHEN the user navigates to `/dashboard` (not project-scoped)
- THEN `sessionStorage.active_project_id` is removed

#### Scenario: Draft cleared on project switch

- GIVEN a draft exists in localStorage
- AND `sessionStorage.active_project_id = "abc123"`
- WHEN the user switches to project `"xyz789"` via project switcher
- THEN the system calls `clearDraft()` which removes `sessionStorage.active_project_id`
- AND the draft is discarded

#### Scenario: clearDraft removes active_project_id

- GIVEN any draft is cleared via `TestPlanContext.clearDraft()`
- THEN `sessionStorage.removeItem('active_project_id')` is called
- AND `data.plan.project_id` is reset to initial state

---

## Success Criteria

| ID | Criterion |
|----|-----------|
| SC-1 | User creates test plan from project-scoped dashboard → plan has correct `project_id` |
| SC-2 | User navigates wizard steps → each step URL contains `?project={projectId}` |
| SC-3 | User switches projects → stale draft is discarded, no cross-project pollution |
| SC-4 | User creates from dashboard (no project) → sees project selector, no stale context |
| SC-5 | Back button from ModeratorGuidePage → navigates to correct project-scoped Registro |

## Affected Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `DashboardPage.tsx` | `handleNewPlan` passes `?project=` when projectId available |
| `NavigationStepper.tsx` | All step navigations include projectId from sessionStorage |
| `NavigationButtons.tsx` | `getTargetPath` injects projectId for all wizard steps |
| `Layout.tsx` | Wizard nav links use `currentProjectId`-prefixed paths |
| `TestPlanContext.tsx` | `clearDraft` clears `sessionStorage.active_project_id` |
| `TestPlanFormPage.tsx` | Draft validation rejects mismatched projectId; sets project_id from context |

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Breaking edit-flow navigation (`handleEdit` uses direct navigate) | Low | `handleEdit` targets `/registro` without projectId prefix; unchanged paths remain functional |
| `sessionStorage` unavailable (private browsing) | Low | All fixes fall back to non-prefixed paths if `active_project_id` is absent |

# Apply Progress: fix/project-id-test-creation

## Implemented 7 Tasks

### 1. DashboardPage.tsx (line 475-479)
- `handleNewPlan()` now clears `sessionStorage.removeItem('active_project_id')` before navigating to `/dashboard/plan`
- Prevents stale project context from polluting creation flow

### 2. TestPlanFormPage.tsx (lines 26-40)
- Rewrote the mount useEffect to:
  - Get `contextProjectId` from `?project=` URL param or `sessionStorage.active_project_id`
  - If missing → set `blocked(true)`
  - If draft exists AND `draft.project_id !== contextProjectId` → call `clearDraft()` and set `blocked(true)`
  - If no `project_id` set → call `updatePlan('project_id', contextProjectId)`
  - Added deps: `[hasDraft, data.plan.project_id, searchParams, updatePlan, clearDraft]`

### 3. TestPlanContext.tsx (line 112)
- `clearDraft()` now calls `sessionStorage.removeItem('active_project_id')` in addition to localStorage cleanup

### 4. NavigationStepper.tsx (lines 36-52)
- `handleNavigate()` now reads `sessionStorage.getItem('active_project_id')` and injects `?project=` query param on target path for all navigation (forward and backward)

### 5. NavigationButtons.tsx (lines 53-58)
- `getTargetPath()` now injects `?project=` query param for wizard steps (not dashboard)
- Reads from `sessionStorage.getItem('active_project_id')`

### 6. ModeratorGuidePage.tsx (lines 43-50, 383-389)

- Back button (line 383) also uses `?project=` query param when navigating to `/dashboard/plan`

### 7. Layout.tsx (lines 272-285)

observations.title: Fixed project context loss on test creation/edition
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed a routing bug where project context was lost when creating or editing a test plan from the dashboard.
**Why**: The `handleNewPlan` and `handleEdit` functions in `DashboardPage.tsx` were clearing `active_project_id` from `sessionStorage` or not passing it to the next page, leading to a "Blocked" state in `TestPlanFormPage`.
**Where**: `src/presentation/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`
**Learned**: Always preserve contextual identifiers (like `projectId`) when navigating to creation/edition forms that depend on that context. Explicitly passing them via query parameters is safer than relying solely on `sessionStorage` which might be cleared by other parts of the app.

observations.title: Session summary: hci-sw-05
observations.content:
## Goal
Fix a visual/routing bug where the project context was lost when navigating from a project-specific dashboard to create a new test plan.

## Instructions
The user wants the "Crear un test" button in the project dashboard to correctly carry over the project context so they are not blocked in the creation form.

## Discoveries
- `DashboardPage.tsx` was being used for both the main dashboard and project-specific dashboards (via `/dashboard/project/:projectId`).
- The `handleNewPlan` function in `DashboardPage.tsx` was explicitly clearing `active_project_id` from `sessionStorage`, causing the next page (`TestPlanFormPage`) to lose context and block the user.
- `handleEdit` in the same file was also missing the context preservation logic.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Modified `src/presentation/pages/DashboardPage.tsx` to preserve `projectId` in `handleNewPlan`.
- ✅ Updated `handleNewPlan` to navigate with a `?project=ID` query parameter.
- ✅ Modified `handleEdit` in `DashboardPage.tsx` to set `active_project_id` and use the query parameter.
- ✅ Verified that `ProjectDetailPage.tsx` already had correct context handling.
- ✅ Verified that `Layout.tsx` (navbar) correctly handles `currentProjectId`.

## Next Steps
- The user should verify the fix in their Vercel deployment after a new build.

## Relevant Files
- src/presentation/pages/DashboardPage.tsx — Fixed `handleNewPlan` and `handleEdit` to preserve project context.
- src/presentation/pages/TestPlanFormPage.tsx — Verified its logic for blocking/unblocking based on context.
- src/presentation/routes/AppRouter.tsx — Reviewed routing structure.

## Instructions
- Los workers deben residir únicamente en `WorkerModule`, nunca ser importados por `AppModule` para evitar su ejecución en el proceso de la API.

- ✅ Realizado commit y push a la rama `feat/student-ai-feedback-module`.

## Next Steps
